title: README
text:
This corpus was generated for vector database and RAG testing.
Each markdown document contains exactly 100 lines.
The set is intentionally redundant and semantically overlapping.

Folders:
- pricing_policies
- service_catalog
- internal_playbooks
- negotiation_rules
- case_studies
- shared_taxonomy

Suggested experiments:
- compare chunk sizes
- compare overlaps
- compare lexical vs semantic retrieval
- test metadata filtering
- evaluate known-answer queries against retrieved supporting documents

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
A regional law firm with offices in three cities contacted the agency after noticing a steady drop in calls from Google search results.
Historically the firm generated most new clients through local pack visibility.
For several years their listings appeared consistently within the top three positions.
During the previous six months that visibility began declining across multiple locations.
The marketing coordinator suspected competitors were gaining an advantage.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
Keyword tracking tools showed a decline in local pack rankings.
Queries such as "family lawyer near me" and "estate planning attorney" dropped from top positions to page two.
Google Business Profile insights also showed fewer direction requests and phone calls.
Organic traffic to location pages declined by roughly 28 percent.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
Analysts reviewed the firm's Google Business Profile listings.
Several listings lacked updated categories and service descriptions.
The firm had very few recent customer reviews.
Competitors in the same market had accumulated hundreds of reviews and posted regular updates.
Location landing pages also contained minimal geographic content.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
Google Search Console revealed declining impressions for location‑based keywords.
Local landing pages had weak internal linking from the main site navigation.
Structured data for local business entities was missing from the site.
Page titles also lacked city‑specific keyword variations.
These factors weakened local relevance signals.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
The team concluded that weakened local prominence signals were responsible.
Competitors had stronger review activity and more complete listings.
The firm's outdated profiles likely reduced their eligibility for local pack positions.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
Local SEO foundation work
Google Business Profile optimization
Location page content improvements
Review acquisition strategy
Citation consistency cleanup

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
Analysts standardized business categories across all listings.
Service descriptions were rewritten to include primary legal specialties.
Images were added to listings to improve engagement.
Location pages were expanded with city‑specific legal content.
Structured data markup was added for local business entities.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
Within three months rankings improved across several high‑value keywords.
Google Business Profile insights showed a 45 percent increase in calls.
Direction requests also increased significantly.
Organic traffic to location pages recovered to previous levels.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
Local SEO performance depends heavily on listing completeness and review activity.
Professional service firms often neglect profile maintenance.
Regular audits of local listings can prevent slow visibility decline.

title: CASE 001 local law firm visibility drop
text:
The same pattern frequently appears in local service industries.
Profiles created during initial marketing efforts often become outdated.
Competitors who maintain active listings gradually gain visibility advantage.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
A fast-growing SaaS company providing project management tools contacted the agency after experiencing a sudden drop in organic visibility.
The company relied heavily on inbound marketing and search traffic for customer acquisition.
Their product documentation and feature pages had historically ranked well for competitive queries.
Six months after a major website redesign the marketing team noticed a steady decline in search impressions.
Organic signups began falling as fewer product pages appeared in search results.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
Google Search Console reported a growing number of excluded pages.
Many product and documentation URLs were categorized as "Crawled – currently not indexed".
Several newly launched feature pages had never appeared in search results.
Organic impressions declined by nearly 35 percent within three months.
Internal analytics confirmed fewer organic trial signups during the same period.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
A technical crawl revealed thousands of URLs across marketing, documentation, and blog sections.
Several important pages had inconsistent canonical tags.
Legacy URLs from the previous site architecture were still referenced internally.
Multiple redirect chains increased crawl complexity.
The XML sitemap also lacked several recently published feature pages.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
Server log analysis showed that search engine crawlers frequently visited low-value archive pages.
High-priority product pages were crawled less frequently.
Robots.txt rules were reviewed and revealed an unintended block on a documentation directory.
That rule had been added during staging and accidentally deployed to production.
Additionally, several parameterized URLs created duplicate content variations.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
The team concluded that a combination of crawl inefficiencies and indexing confusion caused the issue.
Search engines were wasting crawl budget on low-value pages.
At the same time, canonical inconsistencies and duplicate parameters weakened indexing signals.
Important feature pages were not clearly prioritized for indexing.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
Technical SEO audit
Indexing recovery strategy
Internal linking restructuring
Canonical tag normalization
XML sitemap automation
Crawl budget optimization

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
Engineers removed the accidental robots.txt restriction from documentation directories.
Canonical tags were standardized across templates.
Parameterized URLs were consolidated to preferred versions.
The XML sitemap generation process was automated.
Internal links were added from high-authority pages to key feature and documentation pages.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
Within two months Google Search Console reported a steady increase in indexed pages.
Several product feature pages regained top-ten rankings for core SaaS keywords.
Organic impressions increased by approximately 30 percent.
Trial signups from organic search returned to previous levels.
Crawl efficiency also improved as fewer low-value pages were indexed.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
Large SaaS websites are particularly sensitive to crawl budget inefficiencies.
Small configuration errors can significantly impact indexing.
Automated sitemap updates and canonical validation should be part of deployment workflows.
Regular technical audits help prevent silent indexing losses.

title: CASE 002 saas site indexing issues
text:
Many SaaS companies experience indexing problems after major redesigns.
Legacy URLs, duplicate parameters, and inconsistent canonicals frequently appear together.
Without proactive monitoring these issues slowly erode search visibility.
Technical SEO governance is essential for maintaining stable organic growth.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
A mid‑size ecommerce retailer migrated its website to a new platform.
The migration was intended to improve page speed and site management.
Shortly after launch the company observed a sharp drop in organic traffic.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
Organic sessions declined by nearly 40 percent.
Several top category pages disappeared from search results.
Search Console reported a large increase in crawl errors.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
URL structures had changed during the migration.
Many legacy URLs returned 404 errors.
Internal linking structures were partially broken.
Category pages also lost keyword‑rich content during redesign.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
A crawl analysis revealed more than 2,000 missing redirects.
Canonical tags were incorrectly implemented on many product pages.
Robots directives prevented some category pages from being indexed.
Search Console coverage reports confirmed indexation loss.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
The migration introduced technical issues that blocked search engine access.
Broken redirects prevented link equity from transferring to new URLs.
Thin category content weakened relevance signals.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
Technical SEO audit
Redirect mapping reconstruction
Category content refresh
Ongoing technical monitoring

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
Analysts rebuilt redirect mappings from historical URLs.
Canonical tags were corrected across the site.
Category descriptions were rewritten with optimized keywords.
Internal links were restored between major category sections.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
Within two months indexed pages increased significantly.
Organic traffic gradually recovered to pre‑migration levels.
Several high‑value category keywords returned to page one results.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
Website migrations carry significant SEO risk.
Detailed redirect planning is essential before launch.
Post‑migration audits should always be performed quickly.

title: CASE 003 ecommerce traffic drop after migration
text:
Many ecommerce migrations unintentionally break indexing signals.
Careful testing before deployment can prevent most of these problems.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
A regional plumbing company relied heavily on local search leads.
Despite strong service quality, the company appeared inconsistently in local pack results.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
Search queries for plumbing services showed competitors dominating map results.
The client’s profile appeared only occasionally in local packs.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
Google Business Profile lacked service descriptions and images.
Only a small number of reviews existed compared with competitors.
The business categories were also overly generic.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
GBP insights showed low engagement rates.
Search queries containing service keywords rarely triggered the listing.
Competitor profiles included more detailed descriptions and photos.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text: Weak prominence and relevance signals prevented consistent local pack visibility.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
GBP optimization
Service description updates
Photo additions
Review acquisition campaign

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
Profile descriptions were rewritten with relevant service keywords.
Photos of completed projects were uploaded.
Customers were encouraged to submit reviews after completed jobs.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
Within eight weeks the profile began appearing more consistently in local results.
Calls from GBP increased by roughly 35 percent.

title: CASE 004 home services gbp incomplete
text:
Local service companies often overlook profile completeness.
Regular updates improve both ranking signals and user engagement.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
A B2B software provider relied heavily on blog content for organic lead generation.
Traffic had been stable for several years before gradually declining.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
Keyword rankings slowly dropped across many informational pages.
Organic traffic declined about 30 percent over one year.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
Many articles had not been updated in several years.
Competitors published more recent and comprehensive resources.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
Content analysis showed outdated statistics and weak internal linking.
Search Console revealed declining impressions for long‑tail keywords.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text: Content decay reduced relevance for competitive informational queries.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
Keyword research refresh
Content updates
On‑page optimization improvements

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
Analysts updated outdated statistics and examples.
New internal links were added between related articles.
Page titles and headings were optimized with updated keyword research.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
Several updated articles returned to page one rankings.
Organic traffic increased within three months.

title: CASE 005 b2b rankings loss with content decay
text:
Content requires periodic updates to remain competitive.
Informational search results reward freshness and depth.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
A national ecommerce brand experienced unusual ranking volatility.
The internal marketing team suspected link‑related issues.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
Several competitive keywords fluctuated significantly in ranking.
Organic traffic showed irregular declines during algorithm updates.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
Backlink analysis revealed thousands of links from low‑quality directories.
Many links appeared to originate from automated link networks.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
Link profile metrics indicated an abnormal ratio of exact‑match anchor text.
Spam score indicators were unusually high compared with competitors.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text: The backlink profile posed a potential risk for manual penalties.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
Backlink audit
Toxic link identification
Disavow file preparation
Ongoing monitoring

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
Analysts categorized links based on quality and relevance.
Toxic domains were compiled into a disavow file submitted through Search Console.
Monitoring was implemented to track new inbound links.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
Ranking volatility stabilized after several months.
The site avoided potential manual penalties.

title: CASE 006 backlink toxicity and manual action risk
text:
Aggressive link building campaigns can introduce long‑term risk.
Regular backlink audits help maintain a healthy link profile.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
This playbook is activated when a client reports a noticeable decline in organic search rankings.
Typical indicators include reduced keyword positions, falling impressions, or declining organic traffic.
Clients often notice ranking losses when previously high-performing keywords begin slipping to lower search positions.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Verify the decline using reliable ranking data sources.
Review keyword tracking reports across the previous three to six months.
Confirm whether the decline affects a small group of keywords or entire sections of the site.
Check whether the decline aligns with a known search engine algorithm update.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Ask the client whether recent changes occurred on the website.
Examples include redesigns, CMS migrations, or major content updates.
Confirm whether new competitors entered the market recently.
Determine whether new marketing campaigns changed site structure or navigation.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Review Google Search Console for impression and ranking changes.
Examine analytics platforms for organic traffic patterns.
Use keyword tracking tools to measure ranking volatility.
Compare competitor rankings to identify emerging competitors.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Rankings often drop after technical website changes.
Broken internal links and redirect issues may disrupt ranking signals.
Content updates that remove key topics can also reduce relevance.
Algorithm updates may reward competitor content with stronger topical authority.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Technical SEO audit.
Keyword opportunity analysis.
Content optimization and expansion.
Internal linking improvements.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Identify pages losing the most rankings.
Analyze competing pages now ranking above the client.
Expand content depth and improve topical coverage.
Improve internal links pointing to affected pages.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
If rankings continue declining after optimization, escalate the issue.
Investigate crawl errors, indexing issues, and algorithm penalties.
Coordinate with technical SEO specialists to identify deeper structural issues.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Multiple keywords dropping several positions simultaneously.
Organic traffic decreasing across multiple landing pages.
Competitors suddenly outranking established pages.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Ranking recovery typically occurs after improving page relevance and internal linking.
Search engines gradually reassess improved pages.
Ranking recovery may take several weeks depending on crawl frequency.

title: PB 001 rankings loss playbook
text:
Organic rankings fluctuate naturally over time.
However sustained ranking loss usually indicates structural or competitive changes.
Regular monitoring helps detect early warning signals before traffic losses become severe.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
This playbook is used when a client reports a measurable decline in organic website traffic.
Typical symptoms include fewer visits from search engines, declining impressions in Google Search Console, or reduced lead generation from organic channels.
Clients often detect this problem when marketing dashboards show a downward trend in monthly traffic.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Confirm the decline using analytics platforms such as Google Analytics.
Compare organic traffic performance over the past three to six months.
Verify whether the decline is gradual or sudden.
Determine whether the traffic drop affects the entire website or only specific pages.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Ask the client whether major website changes occurred recently.
Examples include site redesigns, content restructuring, or CMS migrations.
Determine whether new competitors have entered the market.
Ask whether recent marketing campaigns shifted focus away from organic search.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Review Google Search Console impression and click data.
Analyze analytics reports for landing pages that lost traffic.
Compare traffic patterns before and after the decline began.
Evaluate keyword ranking reports for major position changes.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Organic traffic declines often occur due to ranking losses.
Technical SEO problems may also reduce crawlability or indexing.
Content that becomes outdated or less relevant can lose visibility.
Competitors publishing stronger or more comprehensive content may also capture traffic.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Technical SEO audit.
Keyword research and opportunity analysis.
Content optimization and expansion.
Internal linking improvements.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Identify pages with the largest traffic losses.
Analyze competing pages that now rank higher in search results.
Expand and refresh existing content to increase relevance.
Improve internal links pointing to affected landing pages.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
If traffic continues declining after optimization, investigate deeper technical problems.
Review crawl errors, indexing status, and page performance.
Escalate complex technical problems to engineering teams.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Gradual decrease in organic impressions.
Landing pages losing traffic despite stable rankings previously.
Competitors gaining traffic for important keywords.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Traffic recovery typically occurs after improving content quality and topical coverage.
Search engines reassess improved pages after recrawling.
Traffic growth usually returns gradually rather than immediately.

title: PB 002 organic traffic decline playbook
text:
Organic traffic trends fluctuate naturally over time.
However sustained declines typically signal structural SEO problems.
Continuous monitoring helps detect issues before they significantly impact revenue.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
This playbook is used when a client reports reduced visibility in local search results.
Typical symptoms include disappearance from the local pack, fewer map impressions, or declining calls from Google Business Profile.
Local businesses often notice this when competitors begin appearing above them for geographic searches.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Confirm that the decline is real by reviewing Google Business Profile insights.
Check changes in impressions, calls, and direction requests.
Compare performance over the previous three to six months.
Verify whether multiple locations are affected or only a single listing.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Ask whether the business recently changed:
- address or phone number
- service categories
- operating hours
- website landing pages
Location data inconsistencies often reduce local search relevance.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Review Google Business Profile dashboards for engagement metrics.
Examine search queries triggering the listing.
Check citation listings across directories such as Yelp and Bing.
Use local rank tracking tools to measure pack visibility.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Local visibility declines often occur due to incomplete listings.
Other causes include inconsistent citations, missing categories, and outdated business descriptions.
Competitors may also accumulate stronger review signals.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Local SEO foundation audit.
Google Business Profile optimization.
Citation consistency cleanup.
Location page improvements with geographic keywords.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Update business categories and service descriptions.
Add photos, services, and updated contact details.
Encourage satisfied customers to leave reviews.
Ensure NAP information remains consistent across directories.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
If visibility does not recover after optimization, investigate deeper issues.
These may include website authority problems or broader algorithm shifts.
Escalate complex cases to technical SEO specialists.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Reduced local pack impressions.
Declining map visibility for high-value keywords.
Lower call volume reported in profile insights.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Most local declines recover after profile optimization and citation cleanup.
Results typically appear within several weeks as search engines re-evaluate listing signals.

title: PB 003 local visibility decline playbook
text:
Local search rankings depend heavily on profile completeness and review activity.
Regular monitoring helps prevent gradual declines.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Use this playbook when a website experiences sudden technical issues affecting search visibility.
Examples include server outages, indexing failures, or widespread crawl errors.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Verify site availability using uptime monitoring tools.
Check whether pages return proper HTTP status codes.
Confirm that the homepage and critical pages load successfully.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Determine whether recent deployments or infrastructure changes occurred.
Ask whether hosting providers or content management systems were modified.
Technical incidents often follow configuration updates.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Server logs reveal crawling errors and response codes.
Search Console coverage reports may show indexing failures.
Crawl tools identify redirect loops or missing pages.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Technical incidents may arise from:
- server misconfiguration
- incorrect robots directives
- accidental noindex tags
- broken redirect rules
Infrastructure changes are frequent causes.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Technical SEO audit.
Indexing remediation.
Server configuration review.
Monitoring implementation.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Restore site availability if outages exist.
Remove incorrect indexing directives.
Fix redirects and canonical tags.
Submit corrected pages for reindexing.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Large outages require coordination with infrastructure teams.
Escalate persistent crawl failures to development specialists.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Sudden drop in indexed pages.
Search Console alerts for coverage errors.
Large spikes in crawl failures.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text:
Technical issues usually recover quickly once corrected.
Search engines typically restore indexing within several days.

title: PB 004 technical incident response playbook
text: Monitoring systems should remain active to detect future incidents early.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
This playbook is activated when website traffic remains stable but conversions decline.
Examples include fewer purchases, lead submissions, or completed contact forms.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Confirm conversion tracking accuracy in analytics systems.
Verify that forms and checkout processes function correctly.
Check whether recent design changes affected usability.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Ask whether the client changed pricing, messaging, or landing page layouts.
Determine whether traffic sources have shifted toward lower-intent audiences.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Analytics platforms reveal funnel drop-off points.
Heatmap tools show user interaction patterns.
Session recordings may highlight usability problems.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Conversion declines often occur due to confusing page layouts.
Slow page speed can also discourage users.
Poor alignment between keywords and landing page content may reduce conversions.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Conversion path analysis.
Landing page optimization.
User experience improvements.
Performance optimization.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Simplify conversion forms.
Clarify calls to action.
Improve page load times.
Align messaging with user intent.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Complex usability issues may require design specialists.
Major checkout failures require developer intervention.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text:
Stable traffic but reduced leads.
High bounce rates on landing pages.
Abandoned checkout processes.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text: Conversion improvements typically appear quickly once friction points are removed.

title: PB 005 conversion drop playbook
text: Conversion analysis should focus on user behavior rather than traffic volume alone.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Use this playbook when a client suspects harmful backlinks affecting search rankings.
Symptoms include ranking volatility or warnings from Search Console.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Review backlink profile metrics using link analysis tools.
Check anchor text distribution for unnatural patterns.
Identify sudden spikes in low-quality links.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Determine whether the client previously engaged in aggressive link-building campaigns.
Ask whether automated directory submissions or link networks were used.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Backlink analysis tools reveal domain quality and link sources.
Search Console link reports show inbound link trends.
Spam scoring tools highlight suspicious domains.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Link networks generating artificial backlinks.
Low-quality directory listings.
Negative SEO attempts from competitors.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Backlink audit.
Toxic link identification.
Disavow file preparation.
Ongoing link monitoring.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Classify backlinks by quality.
Compile toxic domains into disavow file.
Submit disavow file through Search Console.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text: Severe cases may require extended monitoring or reconsideration requests if penalties occur.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Large number of exact-match anchor links.
Backlinks from unrelated foreign domains.
Rapid spikes in new inbound links.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text:
Search engines gradually discount harmful links after disavow submission.
Ranking stability usually improves over time.

title: PB 006 backlink risk recovery playbook
text: Regular backlink audits reduce long-term risk.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
This document defines the general principles guiding price negotiations
between the agency and prospective clients. Negotiations should protect
the integrity of the agency’s services while maintaining flexibility
for legitimate budget constraints.

The goal is not simply to lower prices but to structure engagements
that still deliver meaningful outcomes for the client.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
Negotiations should always preserve the effectiveness of the work.
Reducing price by removing critical analysis or planning activities
often results in ineffective outcomes.

Instead of removing key services, the scope of execution should be
adjusted to fit the available budget.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
Sales teams should begin negotiations by explaining the value of the
recommended services. Clients often focus on price before fully
understanding the work involved.

Clear explanations of diagnostic work, planning steps, and execution
effort help clients appreciate why certain services are necessary.

Clients who understand the process are more likely to accept realistic
project scopes.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
When clients cannot support the full recommended engagement, the first
option should be scope adjustment rather than price reduction.

Examples include:
reducing the number of pages optimized
limiting the number of keyword clusters analyzed
focusing on priority sections of the website

This approach keeps the strategic structure of the work intact.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
Many negotiations can be resolved by dividing the project into phases.

A typical phased structure includes:
initial diagnostic analysis
strategy development
implementation across prioritized pages

This approach allows clients to begin with a smaller investment while
maintaining the long-term roadmap.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
Discounting should be applied carefully and consistently.
Frequent or excessive discounting reduces perceived service value
and creates unrealistic expectations for future engagements.

Sales teams should follow the agency’s discount policies when
considering price adjustments.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
Negotiation discussions can also reveal whether a client is a good
fit for the agency’s services.

Clients unwilling to support essential investigative work may
have expectations that cannot be met within limited budgets.

Identifying such situations early protects both parties.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
All negotiated agreements should be documented clearly.

The final proposal should specify:
services included
services excluded
project timeline
expected deliverables

Clear documentation prevents misunderstandings later.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
Sales teams should be cautious when negotiations focus exclusively
on price reductions.

Clients who repeatedly request discounts without adjusting scope
may undervalue the complexity of SEO work.

These situations should be reviewed carefully before proceeding.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
Successful negotiations create agreements where both parties benefit.
The client receives meaningful strategic work and the agency maintains
the quality of its services.

Negotiations should therefore aim to balance flexibility with
professional standards.

title: NEG 001 negotiation principles
text:
NEG-002 Scope Reduction Rules
NEG-003 Minimum Viable Packages
PRC-003 Discount Cap Policy

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
This document explains how service scope may be reduced when a client’s budget
cannot support a full engagement. The goal is to maintain service effectiveness
while adapting the scope to realistic financial constraints.

Scope reduction should always preserve the logical order of SEO work.
Investigation and planning steps must remain intact whenever possible.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
When a client requests a lower price, the sales team should first evaluate
whether the requested budget can support meaningful results.

Instead of removing essential work, the engagement may be divided into phases.
The first phase may include investigation and planning, followed by
implementation in later stages.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
Certain components of an SEO project can be scaled back without harming the
core investigative process.

Examples include:
- optimizing fewer pages during the initial phase
- limiting content refresh to priority articles
- focusing on the most valuable keyword clusters

Reducing volume rather than eliminating analysis helps maintain quality.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
Certain services should rarely be removed during negotiation.

Examples include:
technical audits
keyword research
diagnostic analysis

These activities identify the underlying cause of a client’s problem.
Without them, implementation work becomes guesswork.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
Sales teams should work with clients to align project scope with available
resources.

Common strategies include:
starting with a diagnostic-only engagement
phasing work across multiple months
prioritizing high‑impact improvements first

This structure preserves strategic integrity.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
Reduced-scope packages should still follow a clear workflow.

1 investigation
2 analysis
3 targeted implementation

Skipping the investigation phase often leads to ineffective work.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
Clients should understand why certain tasks cannot be removed.

Explaining the relationship between diagnostic work and implementation
helps clients appreciate the value of the proposed engagement.

Clear explanations often prevent unrealistic scope reduction requests.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
Sales teams should be cautious when clients insist on removing foundational
work entirely.

Such requests may indicate unrealistic expectations about the complexity of
SEO improvements.

Documenting these cases protects both the client and the agency.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
Effective scope reduction preserves investigation while reducing execution
volume. This approach ensures the engagement still produces actionable
insights and measurable improvements.

title: NEG 002 scope reduction rules
text:
NEG-001 Negotiation Principles
NEG-003 Minimum Viable Packages
PRC-003 Discount Cap Policy

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
Minimum viable packages allow the agency to deliver meaningful value even
when a client has a limited budget.

These packages focus on the most critical diagnostic and remediation tasks.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
The objective of a minimum viable package is to identify the root cause of a
problem and provide a clear plan for improvement.

Even small engagements should generate useful strategic insights.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
Minimum packages often include:

technical site review
keyword opportunity analysis
basic indexing checks
initial recommendations

These components reveal the main issues affecting search performance.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
Clients sometimes approach the agency with urgent problems but limited funds.

In these situations, the minimum viable package allows the agency to begin
with focused diagnostic work.

Once the root cause is identified, the client may decide to expand the
engagement.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
Minimum viable packages help clients understand the value of professional SEO
analysis before committing to a larger engagement.

They also prevent clients from implementing incorrect solutions based on
assumptions.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
The engagement typically follows three stages.

diagnostic analysis  
problem identification  
recommended action plan  

Implementation may occur later depending on client resources.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
Sales teams should explain that the package provides insight rather than a
complete solution.

Understanding the problem is the first step toward resolving it.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
Minimum viable packages are particularly useful for:

small businesses with limited budgets
organizations investigating a sudden ranking decline
companies evaluating SEO providers

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
Although these engagements are smaller, they should still produce clear
recommendations supported by evidence.

title: NEG 003 minimum viable packages
text:
NEG-001 Negotiation Principles
NEG-002 Scope Reduction Rules
PRC-005 Audit Prerequisite Policy

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
This document defines when negotiation decisions must be escalated for
additional approval.

Certain pricing adjustments or scope changes may exceed the authority of
individual sales representatives.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
Escalation should occur when:

discount requests exceed standard thresholds
clients request removal of diagnostic services
the engagement requires unusual scope adjustments

These cases require review by senior strategists or pricing managers.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
Escalated cases should follow a clear review process.

The sales representative documents the request and provides context about
the client’s situation.

A pricing lead then evaluates whether the request can be approved.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
Approvals should consider several factors:

expected project complexity
long‑term value of the client relationship
impact on service quality

The agency should prioritize sustainable outcomes over short‑term revenue.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
All escalated negotiations must be recorded in the project documentation.

Notes should include:

requested change
reason for the request
final approval decision

Maintaining these records improves consistency.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
When escalation occurs, clients should be informed that the request is under
review.

Clear communication helps maintain trust during the negotiation process.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
Occasionally exceptions may be granted when unique circumstances justify them.

However, exceptions should remain rare and carefully documented.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
Escalation procedures protect the agency from accepting engagements that
cannot deliver meaningful results.

They also prevent inconsistent pricing decisions.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
A structured escalation process ensures negotiation decisions remain aligned
with agency policies and service standards.

title: NEG 004 escalation and approval rules
text:
NEG-001 Negotiation Principles
NEG-002 Scope Reduction Rules
PRC-003 Discount Cap Policy

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
This document defines the baseline pricing structure used for SEO engagements.
Standard pricing ensures proposals accurately reflect the effort required to diagnose and resolve search performance issues.

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
SEO work consists of two main phases:
1. Diagnostic analysis
2. Implementation

Separating these stages helps clients understand the value of investigation before solutions are applied.

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
Diagnostic work may include:
- Technical SEO audit
- Keyword research
- Competitive analysis
- Site architecture review

These activities identify the root cause of ranking or traffic problems.

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
Once issues are diagnosed, implementation may involve:
- On‑page optimization
- Technical fixes
- Internal linking improvements
- Content restructuring

Pricing depends on the scope of implementation work.

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
Standard proposals should include:
- Diagnostic phase
- Recommended implementation
- Estimated timeline

This structure reflects the logical order of SEO work.

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
Clients should understand:
- what each service does
- why it is necessary
- how it affects outcomes

Transparent pricing increases trust and proposal acceptance.

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
Pricing may change when:
- the site is unusually large
- technical complexity is high
- the engagement requires custom research

Adjustments must remain consistent with agency guidelines.

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
All pricing decisions must be documented, including:
- services included
- pricing adjustments
- approval notes

title: PRC 001 standard pricing
text:
PRC‑002 Bundle Pricing Policy
PRC‑003 Discount Cap Policy
PRC‑005 Audit Prerequisite Policy
PRC‑008 Diagnostic‑First Engagement Policy

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text: Bundle pricing allows multiple SEO services to be combined into a single engagement package.

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text:
Bundles group related services that support a common objective.

Example bundle:
- Keyword research
- Content optimization
- Internal linking

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text: Bundles simplify proposals and allow coordinated implementation across services.

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text:
Bundles should focus on a specific goal such as:
- content improvement
- technical remediation
- local SEO visibility

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text: Bundle pricing should be lower than the total cost of individual services but still maintain agency margins.

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text: Bundles should include diagnostic work when the root cause of an issue is unknown.

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text: Clients should understand how services inside the bundle work together to improve results.

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text: Bundle performance should be monitored and adjusted when necessary.

title: PRC 002 bundle pricing
text:
PRC‑001 Standard Pricing Policy
PRC‑003 Discount Cap Policy
PRC‑006 Local SEO Pricing Notes

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: This policy defines how discounts may be applied without compromising diagnostic work.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Discounts must not remove critical investigative services required to understand a client’s problem.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Applies to engagements involving traffic decline or local visibility issues.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Discount requests often occur when clients compare agencies or ask for pricing adjustments.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Budget limitations may affect implementation scope, but diagnostic services should remain.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Exceptions may occur if the client provides a credible recent audit.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Large discounts require review by a senior strategist or pricing lead.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Investigating root causes is mandatory before recommending remediation.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: Discounts applied to bundles must maintain logical service sequencing.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text: If a client asks to remove an audit to lower cost, analysts should explain why the audit is necessary.

title: PRC 003 discount cap policy
text:
PRC‑001 Standard Pricing Policy
PRC‑002 Bundle Pricing Policy
PRC‑008 Diagnostic‑First Engagement Policy

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
This policy defines circumstances under which standard pricing guidelines may be adjusted for enterprise clients.  
Large organizations often operate websites with significantly greater scale and complexity than typical engagements.

Enterprise exceptions allow the agency to adapt pricing structures when standard packages do not accurately represent the required effort.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
Enterprise clients typically demonstrate one or more of the following characteristics:

- websites containing thousands or millions of pages  
- multiple regional or international domains  
- complex internal systems or CMS architectures  
- dedicated in-house marketing and engineering teams  

These characteristics often increase the complexity of SEO work.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
Pricing exceptions may be considered when a project includes:

- large-scale site migrations  
- multi-domain SEO strategies  
- international or multilingual optimization  
- enterprise-level technical constraints  

In such cases the scope of analysis and coordination increases substantially.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
Enterprise environments require deeper investigation before implementation work begins.

Diagnostic efforts may involve:

- large-scale crawl analysis  
- log file analysis  
- cross-domain canonical evaluation  
- coordination with engineering teams  

These activities require specialized expertise and additional time.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
Instead of applying standard service packages, enterprise engagements may be structured using custom project scopes.

Pricing adjustments may reflect:

- extended diagnostic phases  
- cross-team coordination  
- additional reporting requirements  
- large-scale implementation planning  

Custom pricing ensures the engagement remains feasible for both parties.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
Enterprise proposals should clearly outline:

- diagnostic scope and methodology  
- implementation phases  
- stakeholder coordination requirements  
- expected project timelines  

Structured proposals help enterprise clients evaluate resource commitments.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
Enterprise pricing exceptions require internal review before final approval.

Sales teams should document:

- reasons for the exception  
- project complexity indicators  
- estimated resource allocation  

Management approval ensures pricing decisions remain consistent across engagements.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
Enterprise projects may involve higher operational risk.

Examples include:

- large infrastructure dependencies  
- deployment restrictions  
- cross-department coordination challenges  

Pricing adjustments should account for these operational complexities.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
All enterprise exceptions must be documented within the proposal record.

Documentation should include:

- justification for exception pricing  
- final approved scope  
- internal approval notes  

Maintaining records ensures transparency and consistent future decision-making.

title: PRC 004 enterprise exception policy
text:
PRC-001 Standard Pricing Policy  
PRC-002 Bundle Pricing Policy  
PRC-003 Discount Cap Policy  
PRC-005 Audit Prerequisite Policy

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text: Defines when a technical audit must occur before implementation.

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text: Search visibility problems can originate from many technical factors.

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text:
Audits are required when:
- traffic declines suddenly
- rankings drop across many pages
- major site migrations occur

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text: Typical audit tasks include crawl analysis, indexation review, and architecture inspection.

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text: Implementation should follow the audit results.

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text: Client‑provided audits may be reviewed and validated before use.

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text: Audit findings must be documented clearly.

title: PRC 005 audit prerequisite policy
text:
PRC‑001 Standard Pricing Policy
PRC‑003 Discount Cap Policy
PRC‑007 Implementation‑Only Policy

title: PRC 006 local seo pricing notes
text: Defines pricing considerations for local SEO engagements.

title: PRC 006 local seo pricing notes
text: Local visibility issues often arise from incomplete business listings or weak location signals.

title: PRC 006 local seo pricing notes
text:
Typical services include:
- Google Business Profile optimization
- citation cleanup
- location page improvements

title: PRC 006 local seo pricing notes
text: Pricing depends on number of locations and competitiveness of the market.

title: PRC 006 local seo pricing notes
text: Local ranking improvements should be tracked after implementation.

title: PRC 006 local seo pricing notes
text:
PRC‑002 Bundle Pricing Policy
PRC‑003 Discount Cap Policy

title: PRC 007 implementation only policy
text: Defines when the agency may perform implementation without diagnostic analysis.

title: PRC 007 implementation only policy
text: Clients sometimes request only technical changes such as fixing errors or updating metadata.

title: PRC 007 implementation only policy
text: Without investigation, implementation may target the wrong problem.

title: PRC 007 implementation only policy
text: Implementation‑only engagements may proceed when scope is clearly defined and low risk.

title: PRC 007 implementation only policy
text: Client acknowledgement should be recorded if diagnostic work is declined.

title: PRC 007 implementation only policy
text:
PRC‑005 Audit Prerequisite Policy
PRC‑008 Diagnostic‑First Engagement Policy

title: PRC 008 diagnostic first engagement policy
text: Establishes that diagnostic analysis should occur before major SEO implementation.

title: PRC 008 diagnostic first engagement policy
text: Traffic loss can result from multiple causes including technical issues, algorithm changes, or content relevance.

title: PRC 008 diagnostic first engagement policy
text:
Typical investigations include:
- technical audits
- backlink analysis
- content gap analysis

title: PRC 008 diagnostic first engagement policy
text:
1 Investigation
2 Analysis
3 Recommendation
4 Implementation

title: PRC 008 diagnostic first engagement policy
text: Diagnostic‑first engagements reduce misdirected work and improve long‑term performance.

title: PRC 008 diagnostic first engagement policy
text: Clients should understand that investigation prevents wasted implementation effort.

title: PRC 008 diagnostic first engagement policy
text:
PRC‑001 Standard Pricing Policy
PRC‑003 Discount Cap Policy
PRC‑005 Audit Prerequisite Policy

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
A technical SEO audit evaluates whether a website can be properly crawled,
indexed, and understood by search engines.

Many search visibility problems originate from technical barriers rather than
content quality. A technical audit identifies these barriers and provides a
prioritized list of fixes.

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
This service is appropriate when a site experiences ranking loss,
indexing problems, or large fluctuations in organic traffic.

It is also recommended before major SEO initiatives to ensure that the
technical foundation supports future improvements.

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
Typical deliverables include:
- crawl diagnostics
- indexing analysis
- redirect and canonical review
- site architecture evaluation
- prioritized technical recommendations

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
Access to Google Search Console
Access to analytics platforms
Crawl access to the website
Server response data when available

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
Common issues discovered during audits include:
- broken redirects
- orphan pages
- crawl traps
- duplicate content signals
- incorrect canonical tags

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
The audit identifies issues but does not automatically include
implementation of fixes unless separately scoped.

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text: The website must allow crawl access and provide basic analytics data.

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
Success is measured by improved indexing stability,
reduced crawl errors, and clearer technical architecture.

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text: Often paired with indexing remediation or on‑page optimization.

title: SVC 001 technical seo audit
text:
Technical audits frequently serve as the diagnostic starting point
for larger SEO engagements.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text:
Keyword research identifies the search queries that users enter when
looking for products, services, or information.

Understanding these queries helps organizations align their content
with real search demand.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text:
This service is used when a website lacks clear keyword targeting,
or when rankings decline due to misalignment with user intent.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text:
Deliverables typically include:
- keyword opportunity lists
- topic clusters
- search intent classification
- competitor keyword analysis

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text:
Access to analytics data
Existing keyword rankings
Competitor websites for comparison

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text:
Research often reveals gaps between the topics a website covers
and the topics users search for.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text: Keyword research does not automatically include content creation.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text: A clear understanding of the client's products, services, or audience.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text: Improved keyword targeting and better alignment with search intent.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text: Often paired with content refresh or on‑page optimization.

title: SVC 002 keyword research
text:
Effective keyword research provides the strategic foundation
for long‑term organic growth.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text:
On‑page optimization improves the structure and content of individual
pages so that search engines and users can understand them more clearly.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text:
Recommended when pages rank but fail to achieve strong visibility
or click‑through rates.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text:
- optimized page titles
- improved headings
- structured internal linking
- updated metadata
- enhanced page structure

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text:
Access to page content
Keyword targets
Analytics and search performance data

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text:
Pages often contain weak heading structure,
missing internal links, or unclear keyword targeting.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text: Does not include full content rewriting unless separately scoped.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text: Keyword research should be completed beforehand.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text: Higher ranking stability and improved click‑through rates.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text: Works well with keyword research and content refresh.

title: SVC 003 on page optimization
text:
On‑page optimization ensures each page clearly communicates
its relevance to search engines.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text:
Content refresh improves existing content that has lost search visibility.

Over time information becomes outdated and competitors publish newer
resources. Refreshing content restores relevance.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text: Best used when pages previously ranked well but traffic gradually declined.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text:
- updated statistics and references
- expanded topical coverage
- improved headings
- refreshed metadata
- additional internal links

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text:
Historical traffic data
Search Console impressions
Keyword ranking history

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text:
Outdated references, shallow content, or competitor pages
with stronger coverage.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text: Does not replace full content strategy development.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text: Pages must already exist and have historical performance data.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text: Improved impressions and restored rankings.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text: Often paired with keyword research and on‑page optimization.

title: SVC 004 content refresh
text: Regular updates help maintain long‑term competitiveness.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text:
Local SEO foundation work ensures a business appears consistently
across local search platforms and directories.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text: Recommended for businesses targeting geographic search results.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text:
- citation audit
- NAP consistency checks
- local landing page review
- directory profile updates

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text:
Business location information
Existing listings and profiles

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text: Inconsistent contact information and incomplete directory listings.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text: Does not include paid advertising management.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text: Business must have clear location information.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text: Improved local search visibility and listing consistency.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text: Often paired with Google Business Profile optimization.

title: SVC 005 local seo foundation
text: Local signals are essential for map‑based search visibility.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text:
This service improves the completeness and effectiveness of a
Google Business Profile listing.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text:
Best for local businesses seeking more calls, visits, or directions
from Google Maps and local search results.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text:
- optimized profile descriptions
- category updates
- photo additions
- service listings
- review strategy recommendations

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text: Access to the GBP listing and business information.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text:
Profiles often lack complete service descriptions,
categories, or recent images.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text: Does not include review generation campaigns unless requested.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text: The business must have a verified Google Business Profile.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text: Increased profile engagement and local visibility.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text: Often combined with local SEO foundation work.

title: SVC 006 google business profile optimization
text: Active profile management strengthens local search prominence.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text:
A backlink audit evaluates the quality and safety of inbound links.

Poor‑quality backlinks may harm rankings or create manual penalty risks.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text:
Recommended when rankings fluctuate unexpectedly or when a site
has previously engaged in aggressive link‑building campaigns.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text:
- backlink quality classification
- toxic domain identification
- disavow recommendations
- link profile summary

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text: Access to backlink analysis tools or link export data.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text:
Large volumes of low‑quality directories,
spam networks, or irrelevant domains.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text: Does not include link acquisition campaigns.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text: Backlink data from analysis tools.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text: Reduced risk of penalties and improved link profile health.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text: Often paired with technical SEO audits.

title: SVC 007 backlink audit
text: Regular link monitoring helps maintain a healthy backlink profile.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text:
Indexing remediation resolves issues preventing search engines
from properly indexing a website.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text: Used when important pages fail to appear in search results.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text:
- indexing diagnostics
- robots directive corrections
- sitemap updates
- canonical improvements

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text:
Search Console access
Site crawl results

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text: Noindex tags, blocked resources, or misconfigured canonical tags.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text: Does not include large‑scale site redesigns.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text: A crawlable website environment.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text: Restored indexing and improved crawl efficiency.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text: Commonly paired with technical SEO audits.

title: SVC 008 indexing remediation
text: Proper indexing is essential for organic visibility.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text:
Conversion path analysis studies how users move through a website
before completing a purchase or lead form.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text: Recommended when traffic remains stable but conversions decline.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text:
- funnel analysis
- user behavior review
- usability recommendations
- friction point identification

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text:
Analytics data
Conversion tracking configuration

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text:
Confusing navigation, slow checkout pages,
or unclear calls to action.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text: Does not include full website redesign.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text: Accurate analytics and conversion tracking.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text: Improved conversion rates and smoother user journeys.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text: Often paired with on‑page optimization.

title: SVC 009 conversion path analysis
text: Understanding user behavior helps improve website performance.

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
The monthly SEO retainer provides ongoing search optimization services
to maintain and improve a website’s organic performance.

Unlike one-time projects, a retainer engagement allows the agency to
continuously monitor search trends, analyze performance data, and
implement improvements over time.

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
This service is appropriate for clients who require continuous
optimization rather than a one-time SEO project.

Typical use cases include:
- sustained traffic growth initiatives
- ongoing ranking improvements
- competitive markets requiring continuous optimization

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
Deliverables typically include:
- monthly SEO performance analysis
- keyword ranking monitoring
- ongoing on-page optimization
- technical SEO recommendations
- content strategy adjustments

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
Access to analytics and search console data  
Access to the website content management system  
Historical SEO reports if available

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
Ongoing monitoring often reveals:
- new keyword opportunities
- emerging competitor strategies
- gradual ranking declines that require corrective action
- technical issues affecting search visibility

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
The retainer does not automatically include large technical
development projects or complete site redesigns.

Major engineering work may require separate project scopes.

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
Initial diagnostic work such as a technical audit or keyword research
is recommended before starting a long-term retainer engagement.

This ensures the agency begins with a clear strategic direction.

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
Success is measured through:
- sustained growth in organic traffic
- improved keyword rankings
- increased lead or conversion volume from search traffic

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
Often paired with:
technical SEO audits  
content optimization services  
keyword research updates

title: SVC 010 monthly seo retainer
text:
Search engine algorithms and competitive environments change
frequently.

A retainer engagement allows the agency to respond to these
changes proactively rather than reacting after performance
declines occur.

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
The concern taxonomy provides a structured vocabulary for describing
SEO problems reported by clients.

Standardizing concern terminology allows analysts to categorize issues
consistently and retrieve the most relevant service documentation.

This taxonomy is used across the agency knowledge base.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
The taxonomy contains several primary concern categories.

rankings_loss  
traffic_decline  
indexing_issues  
local_visibility_decline  
conversion_drop  

Each concern category represents a common pattern observed during
SEO engagements.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Rankings loss refers to a decline in search engine positions for
previously ranking keywords.

Common causes include algorithm updates, content competition,
or technical site changes.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Traffic decline occurs when organic search visits decrease
over a measurable time period.

This concern may originate from rankings loss, indexing issues,
or seasonal search patterns.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Indexing issues occur when important pages fail to appear
in search engine indexes.

Typical causes include crawl errors, noindex directives,
or incorrect canonical tags.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Local visibility decline refers to reduced presence in
map results or local search packs.

Businesses may experience this when competitors improve
their local listings or when citation consistency weakens.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Conversion drop occurs when website traffic remains stable
but lead generation or purchases decline.

This often indicates usability problems or messaging issues.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Each concern category typically maps to specific services.

For example:

rankings_loss → technical audit or keyword research  
traffic_decline → content refresh or technical investigation  
indexing_issues → indexing remediation  
local_visibility_decline → local SEO foundation  
conversion_drop → conversion path analysis  

These associations help guide service recommendations.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Certain policies influence how services are recommended.

Pricing policies determine acceptable engagement structures.

Negotiation policies govern scope adjustments during sales discussions.

The taxonomy therefore links concerns not only to services
but also to related policies.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
The taxonomy acts as the first step in the agency’s diagnostic workflow.

1 identify concern  
2 retrieve relevant services  
3 reference related policies  
4 recommend an engagement plan  

This structured approach ensures consistent recommendations.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Client statement:

“Our rankings dropped after our website redesign.”

Mapped concern:

rankings_loss

Potential services:

technical SEO audit  
indexing remediation  

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
The taxonomy improves knowledge retrieval by providing
consistent vocabulary for indexing documentation.

Semantic retrieval systems can use these standardized
terms to locate relevant documents.

---

title: TAX 001 concern taxonomy
text:
Analysts should assign the most appropriate concern
category before recommending services.

Correct categorization improves diagnostic accuracy
and ensures consistent service proposals.

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
The service and policy crosswalk connects three important
elements of the agency knowledge system.

client concerns  
available services  
relevant policies  

This crosswalk helps analysts navigate the knowledge base
and determine appropriate recommendations.

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
The crosswalk links the following components.

concerns  
services  
policies  
playbooks  

These entities form the foundation of the agency’s
decision framework.

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
The first step in engagement planning is mapping
a client concern to possible services.

Examples include:

rankings_loss → technical SEO audit  
traffic_decline → content refresh  
indexing_issues → indexing remediation  
local_visibility_decline → local SEO foundation  
conversion_drop → conversion path analysis  

This mapping ensures the correct service category is evaluated.

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
Services may reference pricing or negotiation policies.

For example:

technical SEO audit → audit prerequisite policy  
keyword research → bundle pricing policy  
local SEO foundation → discount cap policy  

Policies ensure that service recommendations remain consistent
with agency guidelines.

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
Playbooks provide operational guidance for addressing
specific types of concerns.

Examples include:

rankings loss playbook  
local visibility decline playbook  
technical incident response playbook  

These documents guide analysts during implementation.

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
The crosswalk supports a structured decision process.

1 identify the client concern  
2 retrieve relevant services  
3 review applicable policies  
4 consult playbooks if needed  
5 propose engagement scope  

Following this workflow ensures consistency.

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
Client statement:

“Our local rankings disappeared from Google Maps.”

Concern:

local_visibility_decline

Possible services:

local SEO foundation  
Google Business Profile optimization

Relevant playbook:

local visibility decline playbook

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
The crosswalk document also supports knowledge retrieval.

Search systems can use the crosswalk to identify
related documents across different categories.

For example a query about rankings loss may retrieve:

service documents  
policy documents  
case studies  
playbooks

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
Maintaining a crosswalk improves collaboration between
sales teams, analysts, and technical specialists.

Everyone operates using the same conceptual framework.

---

title: TAX 002 service and policy crosswalk
text:
Analysts should consult the crosswalk when uncertain
which services correspond to a particular concern.

This document acts as a navigation map for the
agency knowledge base.
